title: cuphea congesta
intro: Cuphea congesta, also known as Firecracker Plant, is a flowering shrub native to Mexico and Central America, characterized by its vibrant red, orange, or yellow flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for centuries due to its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Cuphea congesta include its ability to reduce fever, treat wounds, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of Cuphea congesta include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be made by steeping the flowers or leaves in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
